mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in the federal funds rate amplify consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong business investment, which translates to immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth above 0.5% coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate profit accumulation, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, persistent inflation forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength in labor markets and construction demand, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances where both series show positive month-over-month changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of cases where INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously.
